---
title: "Tutorials"
pagetitle: "PyApprox Tutorials | 80+ UQ, Surrogate Modeling & Sensitivity Analysis Guides"
description: "Browse 80+ validated, executable tutorials covering forward UQ, multifidelity estimation, inverse UQ, experimental design, surrogate modeling, and sensitivity analysis."
toc: true
toc-depth: 2
---

::: {.tutorials-header}

[Tutorial Library]{.tutorials-title-text}

[80+ validated, executable tutorials organized by topic. Each tutorial is dual-backend tested (NumPy and PyTorch) and reproducible. Start with the learning path below, or jump to the topic that fits your application.]{.tutorials-subtitle}

:::

## Start Here {.mt-4}

New to PyApprox? Follow this learning path:

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6 .g-col-lg-3}
::: {.start-card}
[1]{.step-number}

[Setup]{.step-title}

[Install and verify your environment](setup_environment.qmd)
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6 .g-col-lg-3}
::: {.start-card}
[2]{.step-number}

[Why UQ?]{.step-title}

[From models to decisions under uncertainty](models_decisions_uncertainty.qmd)
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6 .g-col-lg-3}
::: {.start-card}
[3]{.step-number}

[Forward UQ]{.step-title}

[Propagate uncertainty through a model](forward_uq.qmd)
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6 .g-col-lg-3}
::: {.start-card}
[4]{.step-number}

[Pick a Topic]{.step-title}

Choose a topic below that matches your application
:::
:::

:::

---

::: {.colab-banner}

::: {.colab-inner}

::: {.colab-header}
[Run Tutorials in Google Colab]{.colab-title}

[Every tutorial is available as a Jupyter notebook — download, upload, and run in three steps.]{.colab-subtitle}
:::

::: {.colab-steps}

::: {.colab-step}
[1]{.colab-step-num}

[Download]{.colab-step-title}

Download any `.ipynb` from the [notebooks folder](notebooks/)
:::

::: {.colab-step}
[2]{.colab-step-num}

[Upload]{.colab-step-title}

Open [Google Colab](https://colab.research.google.com) and upload the notebook (File &rarr; Upload)
:::

::: {.colab-step}
[3]{.colab-step-num}

[Run]{.colab-step-title}

Run the first cell to install PyApprox, then run all remaining cells
:::

:::

::: {.colab-links}
[Google Colab](https://colab.research.google.com){.colab-link}
[PyApprox Docs](https://sandialabs.github.io/pyapprox/){.colab-link}
[GitHub Repository](https://github.com/sandialabs/pyapprox){.colab-link}
:::

:::

:::

---

## Forward UQ {.topic-heading}

[Propagate input uncertainty through computational models to characterize output variability.]{.section-desc}

::: {.tutorial-card}
[**Forward Uncertainty Quantification**](forward_uq.qmd){.tutorial-title}
[The forward UQ problem: propagating input uncertainty through a model to characterize output uncertainty]{.tutorial-desc}
[Concept]{.badge-concept}
:::

::: {.tutorial-card}
[**Random Variables and Distributions**](random_variables_distributions.qmd){.tutorial-title}
[Specifying parameter uncertainty with independent and copula-based distributions]{.tutorial-desc}
[Concept]{.badge-concept}
:::

::: {.tutorial-card}
[**Monte Carlo Sampling**](monte_carlo_sampling.qmd){.tutorial-title}
[Estimating QoI statistics by random sampling: variability, sample size effects, and multi-output covariance]{.tutorial-desc}
[Hands-on]{.badge-hands-on}
:::

::: {.tutorial-card}
[**Estimator Accuracy and MSE**](estimator_accuracy_mse.qmd){.tutorial-title}
[Bias, variance, and MSE of Monte Carlo estimators for scalar and vector-valued statistics]{.tutorial-desc}
[Analysis]{.badge-analysis}
:::

::: {.tutorial-card}
[**Monte Carlo Budget Estimation**](mc_budget_estimation.qmd){.tutorial-title}
[Using pilot samples and the estimator API to predict the number of samples needed for a target accuracy]{.tutorial-desc}
[Hands-on]{.badge-hands-on}
:::

---

## Multifidelity Estimation {.topic-heading}

[Reduce computational cost by combining multiple model fidelities with optimal sample allocation.]{.section-desc}

::: {.tutorial-card}
[**Control Variate Monte Carlo**](control_variate_concept.qmd){.tutorial-title}
[Reducing MC estimator variance using a correlated low-fidelity model with a known statistic]{.tutorial-desc}
[Concept]{.badge-concept}
:::

::: {.tutorial-card}
[**Control Variate Analysis**](control_variate_analysis.qmd){.tutorial-title}
[Deriving the optimal control variate weight, variance reduction formula, and unbiasedness]{.tutorial-desc}
[Analysis]{.badge-analysis}
:::

::: {.tutorial-card}
[**ACV Concept**](acv_concept.qmd){.tutorial-title}
[Approximate control variates: extending CVMC to multiple low-fidelity models without known statistics]{.tutorial-desc}
[Concept]{.badge-concept}
:::

::: {.tutorial-card}
[**General ACV Concept**](acv_many_models_concept.qmd){.tutorial-title}
[How using all LF models as direct control variates breaks the CV-1 variance ceiling]{.tutorial-desc}
[Concept]{.badge-concept}
:::

::: {.tutorial-card}
[**General ACV Analysis**](acv_many_models_analysis.qmd){.tutorial-title}
[Deriving the optimal weight matrix, minimum covariance, allocation matrices, and covariance formulas for all ACV estimators]{.tutorial-desc}
[Analysis]{.badge-analysis}
:::

::: {.tutorial-card}
[**MFMC Concept**](mfmc_concept.qmd){.tutorial-title}
[Multi-fidelity Monte Carlo: exploiting a hierarchy of model fidelities for variance reduction]{.tutorial-desc}
[Concept]{.badge-concept}
:::

::: {.tutorial-card}
[**MFMC Analysis**](mfmc_analysis.qmd){.tutorial-title}
[Optimal sample ratios, variance reduction formulas, and numerical verification of MFMC and MLMC]{.tutorial-desc}
[Analysis]{.badge-analysis}
:::

::: {.tutorial-card}
[**Multi-Level Monte Carlo**](mlmc_concept.qmd){.tutorial-title}
[Estimating high-fidelity model statistics using a hierarchy of models by accumulating corrections between consecutive levels]{.tutorial-desc}
[Concept]{.badge-concept}
:::

::: {.tutorial-card}
[**MLMC Analysis**](mlmc_analysis.qmd){.tutorial-title}
[Deriving the optimal MLMC sample allocation, minimum achievable cost, and the MLMC complexity theorem]{.tutorial-desc}
[Analysis]{.badge-analysis}
:::

::: {.tutorial-card}
[**Multi-Output ACV Concept**](multioutput_acv_concept.qmd){.tutorial-title}
[Why estimating several QoIs jointly with multi-fidelity corrections improves each individual estimate]{.tutorial-desc}
[Concept]{.badge-concept}
:::

::: {.tutorial-card}
[**Multi-Output ACV Analysis**](multioutput_acv_analysis.qmd){.tutorial-title}
[Deriving the MOACV optimal weight matrix and the condition under which joint estimation beats independent scalar ACVs]{.tutorial-desc}
[Analysis]{.badge-analysis}
:::

::: {.tutorial-card}
[**PACV Concept**](pacv_concept.qmd){.tutorial-title}
[How a single recursion index unifies MLMC, MFMC, ACVMF, and ACVIS as special cases of one family]{.tutorial-desc}
[Concept]{.badge-concept}
:::

::: {.tutorial-card}
[**PACV Analysis**](pacv_analysis.qmd){.tutorial-title}
[Formal definition of GMF, GRD, GIS families and the covariance formulas enabling enumeration-based best-estimator selection]{.tutorial-desc}
[Analysis]{.badge-analysis}
:::

::: {.tutorial-card}
[**MLBLUE Concept**](mlblue_concept.qmd){.tutorial-title}
[The multi-level best linear unbiased estimator: optimal weights from a single linear system]{.tutorial-desc}
[Concept]{.badge-concept}
:::

::: {.tutorial-card}
[**MLBLUE Analysis**](mlblue_analysis.qmd){.tutorial-title}
[Deriving the MLBLUE weight matrix, variance bound, and comparison with ACV families]{.tutorial-desc}
[Analysis]{.badge-analysis}
:::

::: {.tutorial-card}
[**Pilot Studies Concept**](pilot_studies_concept.qmd){.tutorial-title}
[Using small pilot samples to estimate the covariance structure needed by multi-fidelity estimators]{.tutorial-desc}
[Concept]{.badge-concept}
:::

::: {.tutorial-card}
[**Pilot Studies Analysis**](pilot_studies_analysis.qmd){.tutorial-title}
[Analyzing pilot study accuracy and its effect on downstream estimator performance]{.tutorial-desc}
[Analysis]{.badge-analysis}
:::

::: {.tutorial-card}
[**Ensemble Selection Concept**](ensemble_selection_concept.qmd){.tutorial-title}
[Choosing which subset of available models to include in a multi-fidelity estimator]{.tutorial-desc}
[Concept]{.badge-concept}
:::

::: {.tutorial-card}
[**API Cookbook**](multifidelity_estimation_cookbook.qmd){.tutorial-title}
[Unified API cookbook: one-stop reference for all multi-fidelity estimators]{.tutorial-desc}
[Cookbook]{.badge-cookbook}
:::

---

## Inverse UQ {.topic-heading}

[Infer model parameters from data using Bayesian inference, MCMC, and variational methods.]{.section-desc}

::: {.tutorial-card}
[**Introduction to Bayesian Inference**](bayesian_inference_intro.qmd){.tutorial-title}
[From data to parameters: updating beliefs using Bayes' theorem]{.tutorial-desc}
[Concept]{.badge-concept}
:::

::: {.tutorial-card}
[**Sampling the Posterior with MCMC**](mcmc_sampling.qmd){.tutorial-title}
[Using Markov chain Monte Carlo to generate posterior samples when grid evaluation is infeasible]{.tutorial-desc}
[Concept]{.badge-concept}
:::

::: {.tutorial-card}
[**Practical MCMC: MH and DRAM**](dram_mcmc.qmd){.tutorial-title}
[Tuning proposal distributions, adaptive methods, and convergence diagnostics for MCMC]{.tutorial-desc}
[Hands-on]{.badge-hands-on}
:::

::: {.tutorial-card}
[**Introduction to Variational Inference**](vi_intro.qmd){.tutorial-title}
[Approximating the posterior with optimization instead of sampling]{.tutorial-desc}
[Concept]{.badge-concept}
:::

::: {.tutorial-card}
[**The Variational Objective**](vi_objective.qmd){.tutorial-title}
[Deriving the ELBO and understanding the KL divergence minimization]{.tutorial-desc}
[Analysis]{.badge-analysis}
:::

::: {.tutorial-card}
[**Optimizing the ELBO**](vi_optimization.qmd){.tutorial-title}
[Gradient-based optimization of the evidence lower bound]{.tutorial-desc}
[Analysis]{.badge-analysis}
:::

::: {.tutorial-card}
[**Variational Families**](vi_families.qmd){.tutorial-title}
[Mean-field, full-rank, and normalizing flow variational families]{.tutorial-desc}
[Concept]{.badge-concept}
:::

::: {.tutorial-card}
[**Amortized Variational Inference**](vi_amortized.qmd){.tutorial-title}
[Learning inference networks that generalize across observations]{.tutorial-desc}
[Hands-on]{.badge-hands-on}
:::

---

## Experimental Design {.topic-heading}

[Choose optimal experiments to maximize information gain or minimize prediction uncertainty.]{.section-desc}

::: {.tutorial-card}
[**Introduction to Experimental Design**](experimental_design_intro.qmd){.tutorial-title}
[Why sensor placement matters: comparing designs by posterior covariance and Expected Information Gain]{.tutorial-desc}
[Concept]{.badge-concept}
:::

### KL-Based OED {.sub-heading}

::: {.tutorial-card}
[**OED: KL Concept**](boed_kl_concept.qmd){.tutorial-title}
[What Expected Information Gain measures and why it is the right objective for choosing experiments]{.tutorial-desc}
[Concept]{.badge-concept}
:::

::: {.tutorial-card}
[**OED: Double-Loop Estimator**](boed_kl_estimator.qmd){.tutorial-title}
[How the double-loop MC estimator approximates EIG by treating the marginal evidence as a nested average]{.tutorial-desc}
[Analysis]{.badge-analysis}
:::

::: {.tutorial-card}
[**OED: Gradients**](boed_kl_gradients.qmd){.tutorial-title}
[The reparameterization trick, the C1+C2+C3 gradient decomposition, and the corrected Hessian]{.tutorial-desc}
[Analysis]{.badge-analysis}
:::

::: {.tutorial-card}
[**OED: Convergence**](boed_kl_usage.qmd){.tutorial-title}
[Using KLOEDDiagnostics to measure MSE, decompose bias and variance, and confirm convergence]{.tutorial-desc}
[Hands-on]{.badge-hands-on}
:::

::: {.tutorial-card}
[**OED: MC vs Halton**](boed_kl_qmc.qmd){.tutorial-title}
[Why randomly-shifted Halton sequences reduce MSE compared to i.i.d. MC for the double-loop EIG estimator]{.tutorial-desc}
[Analysis]{.badge-analysis}
:::

::: {.tutorial-card}
[**OED: Nonlinear Usage**](boed_kl_nonlinear_usage.qmd){.tutorial-title}
[Applying EIG-based OED to a Lotka-Volterra predator-prey system]{.tutorial-desc}
[Hands-on]{.badge-hands-on}
:::

::: {.tutorial-card}
[**OED: Design Stability**](boed_kl_design_stability.qmd){.tutorial-title}
[Demonstrating that OED designs depend on MC realizations at fixed budget and stabilize as budget grows]{.tutorial-desc}
[Analysis]{.badge-analysis}
:::

### Goal-Oriented OED {.sub-heading}

::: {.tutorial-card}
[**Goal-Oriented OED: Concept**](boed_pred_concept.qmd){.tutorial-title}
[Why targeting prediction uncertainty rather than parameter uncertainty changes which experiments are optimal]{.tutorial-desc}
[Concept]{.badge-concept}
:::

::: {.tutorial-card}
[**Goal-Oriented OED: Gaussian Analysis**](boed_pred_gaussian_analysis.qmd){.tutorial-title}
[Closed-form posterior mean distribution, entropic risk deviation, and AVaR deviation for Gaussian push-forwards]{.tutorial-desc}
[Analysis]{.badge-analysis}
:::

::: {.tutorial-card}
[**Goal-Oriented OED: Log-Normal Analysis**](boed_pred_lognormal_analysis.qmd){.tutorial-title}
[Deriving the distribution and expected AVaR deviation of the standard deviation of a log-normal QoI]{.tutorial-desc}
[Analysis]{.badge-analysis}
:::

::: {.tutorial-card}
[**Goal-Oriented OED: Convergence**](boed_pred_usage.qmd){.tutorial-title}
[Verifying numerical risk-aware utility estimates converge to analytical values with MC vs. Halton]{.tutorial-desc}
[Hands-on]{.badge-hands-on}
:::

::: {.tutorial-card}
[**Goal-Oriented OED: Nonlinear Usage**](boed_pred_nonlinear_usage.qmd){.tutorial-title}
[Comparing std-deviation and entropic deviation objectives for goal-oriented OED on Lotka-Volterra]{.tutorial-desc}
[Hands-on]{.badge-hands-on}
:::

### OED Workflows {.sub-heading}

::: {.tutorial-card}
[**OED Data Workflow**](boed_data_workflow_usage.qmd){.tutorial-title}
[Generating, saving, and reusing expensive model evaluations so OED optimisation avoids re-running the forward model]{.tutorial-desc}
[Hands-on]{.badge-hands-on}
:::

---

## Approximation Theory {.topic-heading}

[Mathematical foundations for interpolation, quadrature, and polynomial approximation.]{.section-desc}

::: {.tutorial-card}
[**Lagrange Interpolation**](lagrange_interpolation.qmd){.tutorial-title}
[Lagrange basis polynomials, Runge phenomenon, and optimal node placement]{.tutorial-desc}
[Concept]{.badge-concept}
:::

::: {.tutorial-card}
[**Piecewise Interpolation**](piecewise_interpolation.qmd){.tutorial-title}
[Local basis functions for smooth and discontinuous functions]{.tutorial-desc}
[Concept]{.badge-concept}
:::

::: {.tutorial-card}
[**Tensor Product Interpolation**](tensor_product_interpolation.qmd){.tutorial-title}
[Multivariate approximation via Cartesian products of 1D bases]{.tutorial-desc}
[Concept]{.badge-concept}
:::

::: {.tutorial-card}
[**Gauss Quadrature**](gauss_quadrature.qmd){.tutorial-title}
[Optimal nodes from orthogonal polynomials with exponential convergence]{.tutorial-desc}
[Concept]{.badge-concept}
:::

::: {.tutorial-card}
[**Piecewise Polynomial Quadrature**](piecewise_quadrature.qmd){.tutorial-title}
[Rectangle, trapezoid, and Simpson's rules with error derivations]{.tutorial-desc}
[Analysis]{.badge-analysis}
:::

::: {.tutorial-card}
[**Leja Sequences**](leja_sequences.qmd){.tutorial-title}
[Nested points for adaptive interpolation via greedy determinant maximisation]{.tutorial-desc}
[Concept]{.badge-concept}
:::

---

## Surrogate Modeling {.topic-heading}

[Build fast approximations of expensive models using polynomials, sparse grids, and Gaussian processes.]{.section-desc}

### Polynomial Chaos {.sub-heading}

::: {.tutorial-card}
[**Building a Polynomial Chaos Surrogate**](polynomial_chaos_surrogates.qmd){.tutorial-title}
[Approximate an expensive model with a polynomial chaos expansion]{.tutorial-desc}
[Hands-on]{.badge-hands-on}
:::

::: {.tutorial-card}
[**Overfitting and Cross-Validation**](practical_pce_fitting.qmd){.tutorial-title}
[Choose the polynomial degree using LOO cross-validation]{.tutorial-desc}
[Hands-on]{.badge-hands-on}
:::

::: {.tutorial-card}
[**Sparse Polynomial Approximation**](sparse_pce_fitting.qmd){.tutorial-title}
[OMP and BPDN/LASSO for high-dimensional polynomial chaos]{.tutorial-desc}
[Hands-on]{.badge-hands-on}
:::

::: {.tutorial-card}
[**UQ with Polynomial Chaos**](uq_with_pce.qmd){.tutorial-title}
[Extract analytical moments, marginal densities, and convergence comparisons from a fitted PCE]{.tutorial-desc}
[Hands-on]{.badge-hands-on}
:::

### Function Trains {.sub-heading}

::: {.tutorial-card}
[**Function Train Surrogates**](function_train_surrogates.qmd){.tutorial-title}
[Build intuition for low-rank functions and the function train decomposition]{.tutorial-desc}
[Concept]{.badge-concept}
:::

::: {.tutorial-card}
[**UQ with Function Trains**](uq_with_functiontrain.qmd){.tutorial-title}
[Extract analytical moments, marginal densities, and input marginals from a fitted function train]{.tutorial-desc}
[Hands-on]{.badge-hands-on}
:::

### Sparse Grids {.sub-heading}

::: {.tutorial-card}
[**Isotropic Sparse Grids**](isotropic_sparse_grids.qmd){.tutorial-title}
[Smolyak combination technique for breaking the curse of dimensionality]{.tutorial-desc}
[Concept]{.badge-concept}
:::

::: {.tutorial-card}
[**Adaptive Sparse Grids**](adaptive_sparse_grids.qmd){.tutorial-title}
[Data-driven refinement for anisotropic functions]{.tutorial-desc}
[Hands-on]{.badge-hands-on}
:::

::: {.tutorial-card}
[**Multifidelity Sparse Grids**](multifidelity_sparse_grids.qmd){.tutorial-title}
[Multi-index collocation for model hierarchies with varying cost and accuracy]{.tutorial-desc}
[Hands-on]{.badge-hands-on}
:::

::: {.tutorial-card}
[**Sparse Grid to PCE Conversion**](sparse_grid_to_pce_conversion.qmd){.tutorial-title}
[Spectral projection converting Lagrange-based sparse grid interpolants into Polynomial Chaos Expansions]{.tutorial-desc}
[Analysis]{.badge-analysis}
:::

::: {.tutorial-card}
[**UQ with Sparse Grids**](uq_with_sparse_grids.qmd){.tutorial-title}
[Compute moments from sparse grid quadrature, convert to PCE for Sobol indices, and compare against MC]{.tutorial-desc}
[Hands-on]{.badge-hands-on}
:::

### Gaussian Processes {.sub-heading}

::: {.tutorial-card}
[**Building a GP Surrogate**](gp_surrogate.qmd){.tutorial-title}
[Approximate an expensive model with a Gaussian process and quantify prediction uncertainty]{.tutorial-desc}
[Hands-on]{.badge-hands-on}
:::

::: {.tutorial-card}
[**UQ with Gaussian Processes**](uq_with_gp.qmd){.tutorial-title}
[Compute analytical moments, marginal densities, and main effects from a fitted GP]{.tutorial-desc}
[Hands-on]{.badge-hands-on}
:::

::: {.tutorial-card}
[**Adaptive GP Sampling**](adaptive_gp_sampling.qmd){.tutorial-title}
[Choose training points that maximally reduce GP posterior uncertainty using greedy Cholesky and IVAR strategies]{.tutorial-desc}
[Hands-on]{.badge-hands-on}
:::

::: {.tutorial-card}
[**Multi-Fidelity Gaussian Processes**](multioutput_gp.qmd){.tutorial-title}
[Exploit correlations between model fidelities to build accurate surrogates within a fixed computational budget]{.tutorial-desc}
[Hands-on]{.badge-hands-on}
:::

---

## Sensitivity Analysis {.topic-heading}

[Identify which uncertain inputs drive output variability using variance decomposition and screening.]{.section-desc}

::: {.tutorial-card}
[**Sensitivity Analysis Concept**](sensitivity_analysis_concept.qmd){.tutorial-title}
[Variance decomposition and Sobol indices: identifying which uncertain inputs drive output variability]{.tutorial-desc}
[Concept]{.badge-concept}
:::

::: {.tutorial-card}
[**Sobol Sensitivity Analysis**](sobol_sensitivity_analysis.qmd){.tutorial-title}
[Variance-based sensitivity indices for identifying important parameters]{.tutorial-desc}
[Hands-on]{.badge-hands-on}
:::

::: {.tutorial-card}
[**PCE-Based Sensitivity**](pce_sensitivity.qmd){.tutorial-title}
[Computing Sobol indices analytically from polynomial chaos expansion coefficients]{.tutorial-desc}
[Hands-on]{.badge-hands-on}
:::

::: {.tutorial-card}
[**GP-Based Sensitivity**](gp_sensitivity.qmd){.tutorial-title}
[Computing Sobol indices analytically from GP kernel integrals and verifying against fix-and-freeze estimates]{.tutorial-desc}
[Hands-on]{.badge-hands-on}
:::

::: {.tutorial-card}
[**Morris Screening**](morris_screening.qmd){.tutorial-title}
[One-at-a-time elementary effects for cheap parameter screening]{.tutorial-desc}
[Hands-on]{.badge-hands-on}
:::

::: {.tutorial-card}
[**Bin-Based Sensitivity**](bin_based_sensitivity.qmd){.tutorial-title}
[Variance-based sensitivity indices via binning estimation]{.tutorial-desc}
[Hands-on]{.badge-hands-on}
:::

::: {.tutorial-card}
[**FT Sensitivity: Math**](functiontrain_sensitivity_math.qmd){.tutorial-title}
[Deriving Sobol indices analytically from PCE FunctionTrain structure]{.tutorial-desc}
[Analysis]{.badge-analysis}
:::

::: {.tutorial-card}
[**FT Sensitivity: Usage**](functiontrain_sensitivity.qmd){.tutorial-title}
[Computing Sobol indices analytically with FunctionTrainSensitivity]{.tutorial-desc}
[Hands-on]{.badge-hands-on}
:::

---

## Random Fields {.topic-heading}

[Represent spatially correlated uncertainty using Karhunen-Loeve expansions.]{.section-desc}

::: {.tutorial-card}
[**KLE Introduction**](kle_introduction.qmd){.tutorial-title}
[Karhunen-Loeve expansion for representing random fields as truncated eigenfunction series]{.tutorial-desc}
[Concept]{.badge-concept}
:::

::: {.tutorial-card}
[**KLE: Mesh and Data-Driven**](kle_mesh_and_data_driven.qmd){.tutorial-title}
[Building KLE from mesh-based covariance or data-driven empirical covariance]{.tutorial-desc}
[Hands-on]{.badge-hands-on}
:::

::: {.tutorial-card}
[**KLE: SPDE Approach**](kle_spde.qmd){.tutorial-title}
[Stochastic PDE approach to efficient random field generation]{.tutorial-desc}
[Hands-on]{.badge-hands-on}
:::

---

## Optimization Under Uncertainty {.topic-heading}

[Optimize system design when model inputs are uncertain.]{.section-desc}

::: {.tutorial-card}
[**Design Under Uncertainty**](design_under_uncertainty.qmd){.tutorial-title}
[Optimizing system design when model inputs are uncertain]{.tutorial-desc}
[Hands-on]{.badge-hands-on}
:::
